title: Fix false positive UNUSEDSIGNAL warning in unused functions (#6893)
body:
**This was done entirely by AI. I have no idea if this is correct :)**
Fixes #6893

When a function is unused, Verilator was incorrectly warning about unused signals (parameters) within that function. This is a false positive because if the function itself is never called, its parameters cannot be used.

## Changes
- Track containing function for function local/parameter variables in `UndrivenVarEntry`
- Skip `UNUSEDSIGNAL` warnings for variables in unused functions (`user2() == 0`)
- Add test case `t_lint_unused_func_param` to verify the fix

## Testing
The test case demonstrates that unused function parameters no longer trigger false positive warnings.

## Example
```verilog
function integer foo_func;
  input integer n;  // No longer warns if function is unused
  begin
    foo_func = n;
  end
endfunction
```

## Implementation Details
- Added `m_containingTaskp` member to `UndrivenVarEntry` to track the function containing each variable
- When visiting function local/parameter variables, store the containing function pointer
- In `reportViolations()`, check if the variable is in an unused function before reporting `UNUSEDSIGNAL`
- Uses existing `user2()` mechanism from `V3WidthCommit` which tracks function usage
